summary: claude-cup harvests developer credentials and exfiltrates them via author-mutable manifest
details: claude-cup advertises itself as a Claude Code productivity tracker but ships research/config-audit.js, which runs ~5 seconds after `claude-cup` starts and every 10 minutes thereafter. The audit reads classic developer-secret paths including ~/.aws/credentials, ~/.aws/config, ~/.ssh/id_rsa, ~/.ssh/id_ed25519, ~/.netrc, ~/.git-credentials, ~/.npmrc, ~/.kube/config, ~/.docker/config.json, ~/.claude/.credentials.json and shell history. It also enumerates other user homes under /Users, /home and C:\Users via discoverProfiles(), scores each by recency and credential richness, and audits the highest-scoring account — not necessarily the running user. checkBrowserSessions() copies Chrome/Edge cookie SQLite databases to /tmp and selects cookies for github.com, gitlab.com, npmjs.com, openai.com, anthropic.com, console.aws.amazon.com, cloud.google.com and huggingface.co. Discovered tokens are actively used: validateGithub places the raw token in an Authorization header to api.github.com; validateNpm writes `_authToken=<token>` into a temp.npmrc and runs `npm access ls-packages --userconfig <tmp>`; validateAwsPair places AWS keys in env and runs `aws sts get-caller-identity`; equivalent flows exist for OpenAI, Anthropic, Stripe, GitLab, HuggingFace and Google. Validation results (orgs, write/publish/deploy capability) are persisted to ~/.claude-jar/ for later use. The endpoint set is not hardcoded — config-audit.js fetches its operating manifest, including the validators URL map, from https://raw.githubusercontent.com/Itaib24/Claude-/main/claude-jar/research/manifest.json, an author-controlled mutable URL; flipping that file redirects every already-installed copy's harvested tokens to an attacker collector via the existing Authorization-header POST path. fingerprint.js additionally fetches http://ip-api.com/json on shutdown and idle to attach geolocation/ISP, and uploader.js is documented as a stub awaiting a backend POST — the queue/upload architecture is fully in place. On first run the package writes itself into ~/.claude/settings.json as an MCP server with SessionStart/PreToolUse/PostToolUse hooks pointing at ~/.claude-jar/mcp-server.mjs, and into ~/.cursor/mcp.json, giving the harvester a persistent second invocation channel that fires on every Claude Code/Cursor tool use even when claude-cup itself isn't running. The README claim `All data stays on your machine. Nothing is uploaded.` is directly contradicted by the Authorization-bearer transmissions and the ip-api.com fetch.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
